opps.title: Application Development and Support Services - FMCS
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
The General Services Administration, Office of Centralized Acquisition Services, on behalf of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service, is issuing this Request for Quote to establish a single-award Blanket Purchase Agreement under GSA Multiple Award Schedule SIN 54151S Information Technology Professional Services.

FMCS requires Application Development and Support Services to maintain, support, modernize, secure, integrate, and enhance its enterprise application environment. The FMCS environment includes Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform, Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI, Dataverse, LMS365, Azure Government / GCC Medium, custom .NET/C# applications, SQL Server, PostgreSQL where applicable, Collabspace Enterprise Records Management, CCMS, Arbitration systems, the FMCS public web environment, AWS-hosted workloads, and legacy systems.

The scope includes project management, operations and maintenance, minor enhancements, major enhancements, application development, modernization and migration support, cloud migration support, cybersecurity compliance support, ATO/POA&M support, Section 508/accessibility support, records management support, documentation, reporting, quality control, transition support, and related technical services.

The BPA will include the following service CLINs:

CLIN 0001 Project Management
CLIN 0002 Operations and Maintenance and Minor Enhancements
CLIN 0003 Major Enhancements, Modernization, and Migration
The Government anticipates issuing Initial BPA Call 0001 concurrently with BPA award for Base Period ADSS Services. The anticipated BPA period of performance consists of one 12-month base period and three 12-month option periods. The estimated BPA ceiling is $4,500,000. The BPA does not obligate funds; funds will be obligated only through BPA Calls issued by the Contracting Officer.

This RFQ is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside under GSA MAS SIN 54151S. Only quotes submitted by GSA Sch

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Public Relations
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 25-50 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@st

opps.title: Integrating Technology into the Delivery of Clinical Care at Health Centers
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
The purpose of this initiative is to advance the integration of technology, digital innovation (through AI and patient wearable devices) to support more efficient chronic disease prevention and control, with a focus on hypertension, diabetes, and obesity, across HRSA-funded health centers and to reduce health center administrative burden. 
Wearable devices, including smartwatches, rings, fitness trackers, and continuous glucose monitors (CGMs), generate continuous patient health data that, when securely integrated into electronic health record (EHR) systems, equip providers with timely, actionable insights to improve clinical decision-making, close gaps in care, and support value-based reimbursement. 
This contract will also incorporate sister agency initiatives, where applicable, including the Center for Medicare & Medicaid Service's (CMS's) Advancing Chronic Care with Effective Scalable Solutions (ACCESS), Food and Drug Administration's (FDA's) Technology-Enabled Meaningful Patient Outcomes (TEMPO) for Digital Health Devices Pilot, and the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology (ONC) interoperability standards, including the United States Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) and USCDI+ guidance to complement the five pillars of the HHS AI Strategy.
By improving digital and health literacy for both patients and providers, this initiative centers on a patient-focused approach; empowering patients, strengthening shared decision-making, and aligning with HHS's Making America Healthy Again (MAHA) initiative. Through tailored technical assistance, peer learning, and evaluation, this program will help health centers implement strategies that measurably improve outcomes for patients and communities across the country.

opps.title: FTCA Compliant Risk Management Certification Program
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to enable HRSA to fulfill its statutory obligations under 42 U.S.C. 233 by strengthening patient safety, risk management, and operational consistency across FTCA-deemed health centers through a standardized, nationally recognized training and certification program leveraging an existing solution tailored to HRSA needs. This effort will establish clear and enforceable competency benchmarks for risk management professionals, ensuring alignment with FTCA requirements and federal expectations for oversight, accountability, and risk mitigation.
The program will promote consistency in risk management practices, including documentation, credentialing oversight, and claims analysis, improving the defensibility of clinical care and reducing avoidable malpractice exposure to the Federal Government. By equipping staff with the necessary knowledge and skills, it will enhance compliance, strengthen risk identification and mitigation, and support a culture of patient safety, ultimately standardizing risk management capabilities and reducing variability across the Health Center Program.
To establish and deliver a comprehensive, standardized healthcare risk management training and certification solution that leverages an existing, nationally recognized certification program, tailored to meet HRSA and FTCA requirements, and equips health center and free clinic staff in ambulatory care settings with the knowledge and proficiency to effectively manage risk. This initiative supports HRSA's statutory responsibilities under 42 U.S.C. 233 by building core competencies to enable participants to lead, implement, and sustain risk management programs; improving consistency in documentation, credentialing oversight, and risk analysis; and strengthening compliance with FTCA requirements. The program will address inconsistencies in current practices, enhance patient safety, and reduce federal malpractice exposure by improving the defensibility of care. It will

The mission of HRSA/FTCA is to provide programmatic leadership, technical assistance, training, and evaluation while reducing the risk of injury claims and minimizing federal liability for claims filed.

opps.title: Avaya PBX Phone System Replacement for Glacier National Park
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
Glacier National Park (GLAC) currently operates two legacy Avaya/Nortel CS1000 Private Branch Exchange (PBX) systems interconnected via T1 trunks between the Park Headquarters in West Glacier and the Saint Mary Visitor Center complex. The existing infrastructure supports approximately 300 IP/VoIP endpoints (Avaya 1120E handsets), 150 analog lines (serving fax, alarm, and OPX circuits extending up to 24 miles), and voicemail services for approximately 400 mailboxes.

The current CS1000 platform has reached end-of-life (EOL) and end-of-support (EOS) status, so the purpose of this requirement is to procure and install an Avaya IP Office platform at the Headquarters and Saint Mary locations, including 300 Avaya J179 IP phone handsets, software licensing, auto attendant/phone tree and voicemail configuration, 3-year maintenance support, and professional installation services.

This IS a brand name requirement.

This IS a total small business set-aside.

Contract Type: The contract awarded as a result of this RFQ will be a firm-fixed price commercial contract.

Questions are due at least 5 BUSINESS DAYS before the quote due date, making them due by COB 7/21/2026 at the latest.

NOTE: Your company must also be registered in System for Award Management (SAM) at the time of award and throughout the entire award/contract performance - The registration must be "active" in SAM.gov. - Government will award to the technically acceptable offer from a responsible offeror, that represents the best value to the government

*** BE ADVISED - THE PRICE IN GSA eBUY WILL BE THE EVALUATED PRICE ***
CONTRACTING POC: Kiara Stewart - kiara_stewart@nps.gov

The goal of this contract is to procure and install a new Avaya IP Office platform to ensure continued reliable communication services at both the Headquarters in West Glacier and the Saint Mary Visitor Center complex.

opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
The Ohio Army National Guard (OHARNG) is seeking sources for marketing and advertising services for their Recruiting & Retention Battalion (RRB). The objective of this requirement is to establish a contractor-supported digital marketing and lead management ecosystem that enables the OHARNG RRB to modernize recruiting operations by consolidating online recruiting presence, implementing call tracking and routing visibility, replacing Excel-based lead tracking with a CRM, coordinating paid media administration, delivering quarterly market research, automating cold lead nurturing, and providing performance reporting for Government oversight.

The purpose of this announcement is to gain knowledge of potential sources for these services including interested Small Business sources. This notice is for planning purposes only. All interested parties should respond. The Ohio National Guard intends to issue a solicitation and award of a contract for these services.

All interested concerns will provide this office, in writing, with a notice stating their positive intent to submit a proposal no later than July 27th, 2026. This notification, which shall not exceed five typewritten pages, must include (1) positive statement of intent to bid as a prime contractor, and (2) A listing of similar services provided over the past three years, both for government and private industry. The type of service, dollar value, Contract number, location, and Point of Contact are to be included. 

In the event adequate Small Business contractors are not available for adequate competition for this project, it may be advertised as unrestricted. Information shall be provided via email to:
Primary: Claudia Tenaglia, Contracting Specialist, claudia.j.tenaglia.civ@army.mil
Alternate: Herbert Brown, Contracting Officer, herbert.l.brown24.civ@army.mil

Include the following in the subject line "Request for Information (RFI)# 1823475. No facsimile responses will be honored.

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.searchLabel: IRS & Treasury
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.
opps.summary:

The D&PE API Services organization seeks contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain critical middleware applications and shared services

ORIGINAL SOLICITATION POST

The scope of this project is for the contractor to engineer, procure, configure, deploy and install a Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) to reside on a classified network at Goodfellow Air Force Base. In this solution the contractor will provide all necessary software, licensing, hardware (client & server) and on-site support for deployment of no more than 300 zero clients. Additionally, the contractor will optimize network traffic by configuring, deploying and integrating onsite network infrastructure. The solution in its entirety must only incorporate Air Force approved software and hardware. (e.g. Enterprise Approved Product Listing, CCS-3, 2GIT approved devices). All actions will be in accordance with all applicable federal, state, local laws and regulations and this Statement of Work (SOW). 

This is solicited as competitive HUBZone set aside under PSC - DG01 - IT and Telecom—Network Support Services (Labor) and NAICS - 541519 - Other Computer Related Services.

Please carefully review all attachments and solicitation document to inlcude 52.212-1 Instructions to Offerrors and 52.212-2 - Evaluation Criteria. 

Please note that Government is offering a site visit for this requirement and instructions are provided in 52.212-1 of the solicitation.

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.searchLabel: IRS & Treasury
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.title: Equinix Data Center Services Renewal
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
DHS/S&T/CIO requires renewal of Data Center cage space, power, cooling and connectivity for 10 cabinets at Equinix Ashburn and associated wiring and zero hop network connections and internet connection services to DHS Homeland Security Enterprise Network (HSEN), other carriers, and Cloud Service Providers (CSP)s.  The DHS HSEN cage is located in Equinix Ashburn, VA, Bldg DC11.  The DHS S&T Equinix cage also is located at Equinix, Ashburn, Va, Bldg DC11.  
The Data Center Service Provider shall provide the infrastructure support for a Government Furnished Equipment (GFE) Level 1-Basic Services solution.  The Contractor shall continue to provide a colocation facility support for the DHS S&T core infrastructure. The requested facility is located in the DC Metropolitan area that is within fifty 50 mile radius of DHS S&T location (3701 Martin Luther King Ave, SW, Washington DC 20528). The facility shall provide direct zero hop access to DHS HSEN and to carriers such as AT&T, Verizon and Level 1 and CSPs such as Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google, Oracle, and IBM. This direct connection facilitates better access and lower latency to applications within the above-mentioned entities.  Zero hop is defined as providing direct, physical connectivity within the colocation facility campus to HSEN, CSPs, Tier 1 Internet Gateways, and Telcos. This is required to maintain system functionality by reducing network hops and latency, which are caused due to the numerous interfaces between DHS systems, to a minimum. Placing the DHS S&T network systems in a co-location facility also facilitates the routing and inspection of traffic between CSPs, as well as, between the internal DHS S&T network to HSEN and to the CSPs. Additionally, DHS S&T requires space within the Washington DC metropolitan area and direct patching to DHS HSENâ€™s cage. These specialized requirements can only be met by Equinix.

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source Peace Corps' Marketing Campaign 2026-2029
opps.agency: Peace Corps Office of Acquisition and Contract Management
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Marketing Consulting
opps.description:
THIS IS A NOTICE OF INTENT TO SOLE SOURCE, NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTES (RFQ). 

The United States Peace Corps, Office of the Chief Financial Officer, Acquisition and Contract Management (OCFO/ACM), intends to award a sole source, logical follow-on Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) under FAR 8.405-6(a)(1)(i)(C) for Marketing, Communications, Website, and Advertising Production Services.

This requirement supports the Peace Corps’ national-level Integrated Marketing Campaign and is essential to maintaining continuity of creative development, production, and deployment of mission critical outreach activities and volunteer recruitment. The planned anticipated BPA includes a base year and two one-year option periods, with an estimated total value of $30 million. All work shall be performed in strict accordance with the current Performance Work Statement and shall be subject to the terms and conditions of the GSA Schedule contract. 

Under the current BPA, the incumbent contractor is developing the approved 2027 national campaign. If the agency were to compete the follow-on BPA, any new contractor would be required to undergo 6–12 months of onboarding and discovery in advance of campaign development, resulting in unacceptable delays, substantial duplication of cost, and significant disruption to national outreach and recruitment activities. The Peace Corps is pursuing the strategic goal of placing 8,000 Volunteers by 2030. Continuity of national marketing operations is essential to achieving this objective. 

NO RFQ WILL BE ISSUED AND QUOTES WILL NOT BE REQUESTED. Capability statements will not be accepted.

opps.description:
Sources Sought Notice - #2032H326N00011
Financial Management and Business Support Services

1.0	SYNOPSIS
The Treasury Common Services Center (TCSC) provides shared acquisition and operational support services across the Department of the Treasury (Treasury). As part of this mission, TCSC Acquisition Services supports Treasury-wide procurement reporting, analytics, data quality, and acquisition oversight activities.

The U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of the Deputy Chief Financial Officer (ODCFO) requires specialized financial analysis, accounting, and business services to support its business operations.  These services will help improve operational efficiencies, systems and data integrity, and support the implementation of new and current agency-wide business initiatives. Responses to this Sources Sought Notice (SSN) will help ODCFO assess the marketplace for contractors capable of delivering the required services.

The contractor shall provide financial management and accounting support services to include technical research of GAAP, OMB, and legislative requirements, accounting and analytical services in areas such as proprietary and budgetary reporting, variance analysis, problem resolution, data reconciliation, abnormal accounting conditions and business process improvements while also helping to maintain robust internal controls that facilitate Treasury's compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and guidance.  The Contractor must provide all management, labor, and supervision necessary to satisfy Performance Work Statement (PWS) requirements.

The Treasury has not determined the acquisition approach. Any results of this SSN will assist the Treasury Departmental Offices and the Departmental Offices & Enterprise Procurement Branch, TCSC Acquisition Services in determining an acquisition strategy about how to proceed.

This is NOT a formal solicitation or request for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotes. The purpose of this notice is to obtain i

opps.title: A Comparison of Health Care Use and Mortality among Dual Eligibles
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office of the Assistant Secretary for Planning and Evaluation (ASPE), Office of Mission Acquisition Solutions (OMAS) has a requirement for: "A Comparison of Health Care Use and Mortality Among Dual Eligibles in C-SNPs and I-SNPs to Those in Other Medicare Advantage Plans" in accordance with Section C Program Description and Background.  

The Contractor shall independently provide all personnel, labor, facilities, materials, equipment, supplies, and services necessary to perform the Statement of Work, except those expressly identified as Government-furnished.

The study will conduct two quantitative analyses: the first comparing outcomes for dually eligible individuals in Chronic Condition Special Needs Plans (C-SNPs) to those in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and regular Medicare Advantage (MA) plans; the second comparing outcomes for those in Institutional Special Needs Plans (I-SNPs) to those in D-SNPs and regular MA plans. 

The work includes project management, data acquisition through Data Use Agreements (DUAs), development of a detailed work plan and data analysis plan, rigorous data analysis using various government data sources, and the production of research briefs and presentations suitable for policy audiences. 

The Federal Supply Schedule Category "Professional Services," Special Item Number: 541611 and the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code: 541611 "Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services" are applicable to this requirement.  The Product Service Code is: R799 (Other Management Support Services).

opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial items prepared in accordance with the format in FAR Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; proposals are being requested and a written solicitation will not be issued.

This is a non-personnel services contract to provide Wireless internet services to the permanent party dorm campus on Little Rock AFB. The Government shall 
not exercise any supervision or control over the contract service providers performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government.

The contractor shall provide a comprehensive, turnkey, managed bulk Wi-Fi internet service for military personnel residing in dormitory buildings. The purpose of this service is to deliver reliable, high-speed wireless internet access free of charge to the end-users. The solution must cover all designated dorm rooms and common areas, ensuring consistent performance and positive user experience. The contractor shall be responsible for all aspects of service delivery, including network management, security, customer support, and ongoing maintenance.

Please review all attached documents.

opps.agency: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description: The Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC) program is a CMS initiative designed to safeguard the integrity and accuracy of payments made to Medicare Advantage (MA) plans. As the primary mechanism through which CMS validates payment accuracy, the RADV program relies on this contractor to perform and support a comprehensive suite of medical record review activities. These activities include the intake of medical records, abstraction of diagnosis codes, quality assurance and continuous improvement efforts, appeals support, and systems and reporting enhancements all of which work together to ensure that MA plan payments are based on accurate and verified clinical documentation. This task order is issued under the Center for Program Integrity (CPI) Payment Accuracy Acquisition Strategy, reflecting CMS's broader commitment to program integrity and responsible stewardship of federal healthcare funds.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
After the completion of the State Laws digest on illegal passing, a more in-depth review or state of knowledge report is needed to determine if a model law for illegal passing would help with the consistency of enforcement of these driving violations. More clarity if needed on how currently laws are enforced to help better inform states to improve enforcement of these laws. The state laws digest informed that laws vary in content, organization, consistency, and clarity. Also, that what may constitute illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neigh-boring one. States also vary in their methods to review, document and report violations including permissibility of stop-arm cameras. This report is needed to give clarity on how states can best improve enforcement of this law.

To address this need, this project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This study will provide if a model law would be necessary for continuity among enforcement within the U.S States and provide best example of a model law.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is seeking to enhance the enforcement of laws against the illegal passing of school buses, a significant safety concern for students. 

The project aims to address inconsistencies in enforcement and prosecution across various jurisdictions, as current laws vary widely in their content and clarity. A previous digest indicated that illegal passing violations are often dismissed in court, highlighting the need for improved education and advocacy among judicial officials. This solicitation seeks to conduct a comprehensive revie

opps.title: Worker's Compensation Services Contract
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to provide the WCF with a comprehensive Workers' Compensation program that meets the Division of Federal Employees; Compensation (DFEC) Office of Workers' Compensation Programs (OWCP) standards.  The program must abide by all DFEC and other applicable codes. The Contractor shall provide the necessary personnel, supervision and supplies to maintain such a program.  The services to be provided by the Contractor under this contract includes, but are not limited to: 

A summary of workload statistics for specific categories of WC case management; 

Assist BEP Safety Manager in organizing, tracking and facilitating WC claims for the BEP employees; Consult with employees, supervisors and management to provide clarification and educate on workers' injuries and health issues; Provide guidance and information to the BEP Human Resource Specialist (HRS), employees and their supervisors during the WC process; Follow up with physician offices to obtain correspondence, medical documentation and other information needed to support the WC claims process. 

See attached documents. 

Quotes are due August 10, 2026
Questions are due by July, 28, 2026
opps.summary:
Background
 The Workers' Compensation Services Contract is issued by the Bureau of Engraving and Printing (BEP) under the Western Currency Facility (WCF). The goal of this contract is to establish a comprehensive Workers' Compensation program that complies with the standards set by the Division of Federal Employees' Compensation (DFEC) Office of Workers' Compensation Programs (OWCP).

opps.description:
The U.S. Department of the Interior, Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement (OSMRE), seeks quotations from GSA MAS contractors for continued Operations & Maintenance (O&M), ETL/GIS workflow support, system enhancements, legislative/policy update integration, cybersecurity compliance, and transition planning for the Enhanced Abandoned Mine Land Inventory System (eAMLIS).
eAMLIS is a mature, mission-critical system supporting statutory obligations under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act (SMCRA). It provides annually reported AML inventory information to DOI leadership, Congress, States, Tribes, and external stakeholders. System requirements, technical objectives, and operational responsibilities are detailed in the attached Statement of Objectives (SOO).

The Contractor shall provide all personnel, tools, equipment, and services necessary to maintain stable system operations, support ETL pipelines and geospatial features, execute modernization efforts, and ensure timely updates in response to legislative changes and federal audits (including OIG recommendations).

A hybrid Firm-Fixed-Price (FFP) and Labor-Hour (LH) order is anticipated.

The Government intends to award a task order from this solicitation to the responsible offeror whose offer conforming to the solicitation will be most advantageous to the Government, price and other factors considered. The following factors shall be used to evaluate offers: 
Factor 1: Technical Approach (Most Important)
Factor 2: Relevant Experience & Past Performance
Factor 3: Price

The Government will select the best-suited vendor using a comparative evaluation process IAW FAR 80405-2(c)(3). 

Please see attached solicitation (B06 - SOL-eAMLIS) for full RFQ details.

opps.title: Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO) Compliance Assistance Reporting Terminal (CART)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
This task order award under the DHS SWIFT PaaS IDIQ to operate, maintain, enhance, and deploy the Compliance Assistance Reporting Terminal (CART) system in support of ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO). The requirement includes corrective, adaptive, perfective, and preventive software maintenance for CART services and the cloud?hosted CART Portal; manufacturing, shipping, installing, and maintaining self?service CART kiosks at ERO field offices; providing Tier 2/3 technical support; performing required security, privacy, and Section 508 compliance activities; and ensuring all information, data, and functionality can be integrated with or transitioned into the DHS Case Management & Analytics (CMA) system. 

CART is a congressionally mandated, DHS Level III Acquisition Program that increases automation for repeatable processes and reduces the level of officer interaction in the non-detained check-in process.

CART also ensures the appropriate level of case management/supervision for effective oversight of released aliens. As the non-detained docket check-in process can vary among ERO offices, the program also seeks to provide greater standardization across all ERO offices that perform this duty. This helps to ensure more consistency and reliability for ERO Officers responsible for performing these duties and for the aliens who are required to report to a Deportation Officer as a condition of release.

The solicitation will be issued against the Scalable Ways to Implement Flexible Tasks (SWIFT) Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) Collaborative Services Domain.

opps.title: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
CBP has a requirement to procure cloud computing capabilities, Software as a Service (SaaS), Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), and Platform as a Service (PaaS) (Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI)). CBP requires the vendor to enable dynamic consumption of any available service offered (e.g., compute, storage, databases, analytics), by delivering the required number of OCI Units which CBP utilizes for financial tracking and forecasting purposes. This approach will successfully enable CBP to quickly, reliably, and consistently deliver modernized digital solutions. These computing capabilities will allow for building digital solutions based on micro services; implementing API (Application Program Interface) based modern web frameworks; building solutions that are extendable to mobile and forward-looking industry paradigms; building consistent, standard, reliable, and portable environments in the cloud; and defining container strategies and operational models. This effort will require delivery of an efficient methodology that considers the following:  

1.	Provisioning of Cloud Services Provider (CSP) resources â€“ including, but not limited to, the capabilities and services they offer, be it SaaS, IaaS or PaaS.
2.	This effort will not provide direct labor IT services, this effort is solely for the use of cloud computing resources.

Acquisition Strategy: Full and Open on SEWP

Extensive interaction with MCC will be required for the experts to fully understand MCC's procurement documents and/or to advise MCC in modifying these documents as and when needed.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

Additional support and guidance will be provided by regional network engineers and DHA centralized infrastructure team.

opps.title: NSWC Carderock - Common Access Card (CAC) Office Support Services
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Data Processing/Hosting (cloud/data/AI infra)
opps.description: PLEASE NOTE: This procurement will be procured under the 8(a) program and only businesses currently enrolled in the 8(a) program shall be considered. The Naval Surface Warfare Center, Carderock Division (NSWCCD) has a requirement to maintain the current Common Access Card (CAC) support office on site at the West Bethesda, MD location. The CAC support office allows for all military, civilian, and contractor personnel working on site, or for other DoD/Government agencies, to obtain and maintain their CACs. Services include the issuance of CACs, the return of CACs when personnel are no longer eligible, and trouble shooting (such as PIN resets, CAC renewals, and CAC card replacements when a defective or faulty card is identified). This requirement is for an estimated 5,640 labor hours per year, which is split between two separate labor categories as follows: 1,880 labor hours for Project Manager/Quality Control and 3,760 labor hours for two Site Security Managers/CAC Technicians. This requirement will contain a base year with four option years. This Sources Sought notice includes a Draft Performance Work Statement (PWS) and a Market Research Questionnaire. The Government is requesting feedback on draft documents and for market research purposes. Interested parties should respond with a completed Market Research Questionnaire.

THIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTES. THIS IS A NOTICE OF THE GOVERNMENT’S INTENT TO ISSUE A SOLE SOURCE AWARD CONTRACT TO TECOLOTE RESEARCH, INC FOR CADE TECHNICAL SUPPORT SERVICES

The United States Department of War (DoW), Washington Headquarters Services, Acquisition Directorate (WHS/AD), on behalf of the Office of the Secretary of War Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation (CAPE)
intends to award a contract on a sole source basis to Tecolote Research, Inc., 420 South Fairview Ave, Suite 201, Goleta, CA 93117-3654, for CADE Technical Support Services with a one month base period, a one month 52.217-9 option period, and  a 52.217-8, Option to extend Services. The primary North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code is 541519, Other  Computer Selated Services. Small business size standard $34 million.   

The anticipated award will be made under the authority of 10 U.S.C. 3204 as implemented Revolutionary  Federal Acquisition Regulation  Overall (RFO) 6.103-1, Only one responsible source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements. The contract is intended to continue the requirement for CADE Technical Support Services while a new competitively awarded contract is being procured.

This notice of intent is NOT a request for proposals or quotations. Requests for copies of a solicitation in response to this notice will not be honored or acknowledged. A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed action based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government. Information received will be considered solely for the purpose of determining whether conducting a competitive procurement is in the best interest of the Government. Interested parties may identify their interest and capability by responding to this notice via email to whs.mc-alex.ad.mbx.ks@mail.mil , with a file size less than 5MB, Subject Line: Response to NOI DL0710202612572 CADE Technical Support, no later than 10:00 A.M. The RFI seeks input from industry stakeholders on how to regenerate an important part of the American economy by making it easier to buy American products through GSA Advantage. Any questions regarding this RFI can be sent to: rfi@research.gsa.gov. Please include the RFI number in your email title.

opps.title: BPA for Shared Electronic Health Record (EHR) Platform and Support Services
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), under the direction of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), will issue and manage a Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) for an integrated, multi-module correctional Electronic Health Record (EHR) system. Under this BPA, two task orders will be issued: one for ICE Health Service Corps (IHSC) and one for U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP). 

The Contractor shall deliver an integrated, multi-module correctional EHR system that enables secure, interoperable, and longitudinal health records across components, while supporting component-specific operational workflows. The system shall interface seamlessly with DHS enterprise services and external components and stakeholder systems to support evidence-based care, patient safety, and continuity of care. The EHR shall maintain a single, longitudinal health record per detainee across custody transitions (e.g., CBP to IHSC), ensuring data integrity, traceability, and real-time accessibility, in compliance with all applicable federal, DHS, and component-level policies and standards.

The vendor must assess whether the proposed solution provides a holistic, integrated system encompassing all functionalities of the current EHR subsystems. If the proposed system cannot deliver all required functionalities to all facilities covered under each task order by the intended deployment date, the vendor shall identify and engage qualified subcontractors as necessary to address any gaps and ensure all requirements are met.

The proposed solution must be certified by the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology (ONC-HIT). If not already ONC-HIT certified, the vendor shall obtain certification within one year of implementation. The solution must be FedRAMP high authorized or able to demonstrate an equivalent security certification. The solutions must run on ICE GovCloud or receive an ICE Chief Information Officer exception to be hosted in an alternate FedRAM

It is also the only known DHIS2 Platform with a focus on multi-country deployments, custom applications, and systems integration to meet diverse organizational needs.

opps.title: NATO Business Opportunity: Operational Network – Extension (ON-X)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
The NATO Communications and Information Agency (NCIA) intends to issue a Request for Proposal (RFP) for the provision, operation, and continuous evolution of the Operational Network Extension (ON-X), a NATO Enterprise cloud capability, up to and including NATO SECRET, delivered and operated as a managed service.

Potential U.S. prime contractors must 1) maintain a professionally active facility (office, factory, laboratory, etc.) within the United States, 2) be pre-approved for participation in NATO  Competitive Procurement (NCP), 3) be issued a Declaration of Eligibility (DOE) by the Department of Commerce (DOC), and 4) register with the NCI Agency’s eProcurement tool, Neo: https://www.ncia.nato.int/business/neo-eprocurement.html 

The reference for the RFP is RFP-CO-424373 and all correspondence concerning the RFP should include this reference.

SUMMARY OF REQUIREMENT

The Alliance operates multiple NATO SECRET (NS) networks without full interoperability or centralized management, and it is constrained in scalability, resilience, Enterprise-wide collaboration, innovation intake, and cost transparency. ON-X aims to address these current limitations.

NCIA intends to contract a single accountable prime contractor for the delivery, operation, and continuous evolution of a scalable, secure-by-design, General-Purpose Cloud capability that stores and processes data up to and including NATO SECRET. The effort will also include the provision of secure digital workplace services delivered to the NATO Enterprise as a service. The prime contractor will function as a managed-service provider under NATO command and governed by NATO policies and directives.

The requirement is expressed as outcomes, measurable end-states the capability must achieve, and bidders will propose their solution. The outcomes to be contracted span, at a high level include but are not limited to:

• Progressive take-over and management of the operation of the existing Operational Network (ON

opps.title: Facility Inspection & Detainee Death Review Services
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
The Office of Professional Responsibility (OPR) is an organizational element within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The Office of Detention Oversight (ODO) is a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office (ISP). ODO conducts compliance inspections of detention facilities in which detainees are housed for more than seventy-two (72) hours to determine compliance with the ICE 2025 and 2019 National Detention Standards (NDS), the 2025 Non-Dedicated Intergovernmental Detention Standards (NDIDS), the 2011 Performance Based National Detention Standards (PBNDS), as applicable, and the 2020 Family Residential Standards (FRS).
The External Reviews and Analysis Unit (ERAU) is also a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office. ERAU is responsible for conducting administrative reviews of all deaths of individuals in ICE custody. The Detainee Death Review (DDR) is a focused, fact finding, objective review of the facts and circumstances surrounding a detainee's death. ERAU also conducts special and focused reviews, as requested by ICE leadership.
The Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ), on behalf of ICE/ERAU is seeking a vendor who shall provide support services related to inspections and detainee death reviews to determine facility compliance with the NDS, NDIDS, PBNDS, or FRS, and for supporting designated analytical activities within ODO and ERAU.  The vendor will provide expert operational, analytical and administrative support for ODO and ERAU inspections, reviews, and special assignments.
The vendor shall provide productivity and management support, including but not limited to, quality control; data input; clerical support; statistical and analytical documentation and data manipulation; training of personnel; and other related functions. The vendor's project managers for both inspections and DDRs shall perform day to day management of overall contract support operations and ensure conformity with all contract
opps.summary:
Background
 The Office of Professional Responsibility (OPR) is part of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), specifically within U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The OPR's Inspections Program Office includes the Office of Detention Oversight (ODO), which conducts compliance inspections of detention facilities housing detainees for over 72 hours. These inspections assess compliance with various detention standards, including the ICE National Detention Standards (NDS) and Family Residential Standards (FRS).

opps.title: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access planform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components.
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Data Processing/Hosting (cloud/data/AI infra)
opps.description: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access platform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components. The Contractor shall perform research of repair line-item costs in each Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) code areas across the country to provide a database that represents the local economy in a disaster location. Services specified under this contract require quarterly updates and additional pricing work that reflects changes in prices due to current economic conditions in each FIPS code location. Develop and update the FEMA Line-Item Database in a Microsoft Access format with an interactive front-end application capable of entering a disaster number, selecting a state or territory, selecting counties, municipalities or parishes within the state or territory, and exporting the pricing data in FEMAâ€™s designated tab delimited format described in Attachment B. 3.1.1. The line-item format includes a category, line-item number, line-item name, unit of measure and a description of repair represented by the line item. The line-item cost is a sum of all cost components associated in the use of that line item. The database shall be updated quarterly, delivered to FEMA through an FTP (File Transfer Protocol) site to be imported by FEMA. The first deliverable of the base and each option year shall be delivered in the first week of: March=Q1, June=Q2, September=Q3, December=Q4. Develop an annual Line-Item Mapping Master â€“ A Microsoft Excel file of the Line-Item basis and rationale used for pricing that provides a breakdown of the line-item components as CSI codes with national average costs.

opps.description:
SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE

Indian Small Business Economic Enterprise (ISBEE) / Indian Economic Enterprise (IEE) Capability Inquiry

VistA Imaging / Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) Support Services:

The Phoenix Area Indian Health Service (IHS) is conducting market research to determine the availability and capability of qualified Indian Small Business Economic Enterprises (ISBEEs) and Indian Economic Enterprises (IEEs) interested in providing VistA Imaging and Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) support, maintenance, and administration services for healthcare facilities within the Phoenix Area.

This notice is issued solely for information and planning purposes and does not constitute a solicitation or a promise to issue a solicitation in the future. This notice does not commit the Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. Further, the Government is not seeking proposals at this time and will not reimburse respondents for any costs associated with responding to this notice.

Requirement Overview:

The contemplated requirement consists of technical support, administration, and maintenance services for VistA Imaging and PACS systems used to acquire, store, distribute, and display diagnostic medical images (e.g., radiology, cardiology, and other clinical imaging) within Indian Health Service healthcare facilities.

The Government is contemplating establishment of a single-award or multiple-award Area-wide acquisition vehicle, which may include a Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA), Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract, or other appropriate contractual instrument, to support multiple healthcare facilities throughout the Phoenix Area.

The contemplated requirement may support up to seven (7) healthcare facilities; however, no minimum utilization, guaranteed usage, guaranteed quantity, or guaranteed number of participating facilities is being provided under this notice or any future acquisition vehicle. Actual

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Award a single-source contract - Bruker OPUS Software upgrades AFSC
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
NOTICE OF INTENT TO SOLE SOURCE

NOAA's National Marine Fisheries Service - Alaska Fisheries Science Center - intends to negotiate, on a sole source basis, under authority of FAR 13.106-1(b)(1); with the Bruker Corporation, for the procurement of updated OPUS software systems which operate agency-owned Fourier near-infrared spectrometers in Seattle, Washington. Researchers with the Alaska Fisheries Science Center utilize near-infrared spectrometers to analyze fish otoliths and other structures to determine age and rate of fish developmental processes and how those processes are affected by environmental factors (such as temperature and chemistry changes in the marine environment). One way to accurately determine the age of ocean-dwelling fish is to sample and analyze otoliths under controlled conditions. This analytical work is conducted directly in support of Executive Order 14276 – “Restoring American Seafood Competitiveness”.

Sole Source Justification

The sole source determination is based on the need of NMFS researchers to accurately obtain information related to an organism’s age and growth development and how the developmental processes of an organism are affected by conditions in the marine environment. Researchers can then use this data to make recommendations for the expansion of some and reduction of other active fishing areas to support healthy populations of commercially valuable fish species. Researchers utilize the best available science and methods in the generation of accurate data to ultimately provide recommendations to fisheries managers.  

Special Notice

This notice of intent is not a request for competitive quotes and no solicitation package is available. However, firms that believe they can provide equipment or service that fully meets the Government's requirements may submit substantiating documentation to the identified point of contact within 15 days after publication of this synopsis. Such documentation

The research conducted supports Executive Order 14276 aimed at restoring American seafood competitiveness.

opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
The NATO Communications and Information Agency (NCIA) intends to issue a Request for Quotation (RFQ) for procurement of Tier 2 and Tier 3 Uplift of the SIEM.

Potential U.S. prime contractors must 1) maintain a professionally active facility (office, factory, laboratory, etc.) within the United States, 2) hold a Facility Security Clearance (FCL) at the level of SECRET or higher, 3) be pre-approved for participation in NATO Competitive Procurement (NCP), 4) be issued a Declaration of Eligibility (DOE) by the Department of Commerce (DOC), and 5) register with NCIA’s eProcurement tool, Neo: https://www.ncia.nato.int/business/procurement/neo-eprocurement 

In addition, contractor personnel will be required to work unescorted in Class II Security areas. Therefore, access can only be permitted to cleared individuals. Only companies maintaining the FCL and the appropriate personnel clearances will be able to perform the resulting contract.

The reference for the RFQ is RFQ-CO-424357-SIEM and all correspondence concerning the RFQ should include this reference.

SUMMARY OF REQUIREMENT

Project Objective

Tier 2+3 SIEM 3 Uplift of the SIEM incl. build of DEV environment

Design, hardware supply and implementation activities to facilitate the expansion of the existing NCSC SIEM, which includes specific requirements to increase capacity.

Design, hardware supply and implementation of the Development environment and the Reference environment for this SIEM on Tier-2.

Scope of Work

There are three parts to be delivered under Tier 2+3 SIEM 3 Uplift of the SIEM incl. build of DEV environment:

1)    Design and implement the expansion of the existing NCSC SIEM, to include increased capacity.

2)    Implementation of the Development environment and the Reference environment for this SIEM on Tier-2.

3)    Design and implementation to Tier-3 of passive metadata generation in Tier-3 sites and the flow into the existing Tier-2 SIEM.

The contractor will be required to provide all personnel and expertise required to perform comprehensive administrative, personnel, and physical security support to the 89th Attack Squadron (89 ATKS) at Ellsworth Air Force Base, South Dakota. opps.title: 165 ASOS HPO Services
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
Special Warfare (SW)/Tactical Air Control Party (TACP) in the Air National Guard (ANG) must always be ready to perform at their peak in order to accomplish their challenging mission set.  Attaining and maintaining this level of peak human performance requires the implementation of a top-notch physical conditioning and maintenance program comparable to those seen in Olympic-level and professional sports organizations.  The Human Performance Optimization (HPO) program will focus on the whole operator concept to not only assist TACP operators in accomplishing their military missions in the short term, but also to emphasize the maintenance of peak performance over the long term in order to lengthen the service life of the "human weapon system" while ensuring high-quality production.  The contractor shall provide all personnel, equipment, tools, materials, supervision, and quality control necessary, except as specified in Paragraph 3.0 as Government Furnished, to perform Preservation of the Force and Family Services, as defined in this PWS.

Please Note: 
Review all attachments in their entirety prior to submitting a proposal.  To be considered for award, you must complete and sign the SF 1449.  

A Site Visit will not be conducted for this acquisition.

Any and all questions are required to be submitted No Later Than close of business 21 July 2026.  Answers will be provided no later than close of business 28 July 2026.

Please submit all questions to:  justin.smith.173@us.af.mil and andrew.headrick@us.af.mil.

Contract Award (estimated): 15 December 2026

opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY

OFFICE OF TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE



Information Technology Manager



The U.S. Department of the Treasury’s Office of Technical Assistance (OTA) supports the development of strong financial sectors and sound public financial management in countries where assistance is needed and there is a strong commitment to reform. OTA provides technical assistance in over 50 countries in five functional areas: economic crimes, government debt and infrastructure finance, budget and financial accountability, banking and financial services, and revenue administration.



OTA is seeking an Information Technology (IT) Manager to provide a wide range of IT management and administrative services in support of its global projects. The person selected will be hired under a personal services contract. This position is located at OTA headquarters in Washington, DC. This is not a remote position. Some travel may be required.



Qualifications:


	Significant, proven working experience as an IT manager or relevant experience successfully implementing IT strategies and IT security policies, preferably in the public sector
	Technical management experience in computer networks and hardware and software systems
	Expert knowledge of information technology standards and practices to include:
	
		knowledge of IT applications and systems (e.g. operating systems and user interfaces)
		experience implementing new technologies and evaluating implemented technologies
		experience in preparing and testing disaster recovery and business continuity plan
		experience in developing, implementing and enforcing information security and control framework, policy and procedures
	
	
	Excellent written and oral communication skills
	Strong organizational skills and significant project management experience
	Proven experience in strategic planning, risk management, change management and people management
	B.S. in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field is desired

CISA's Office of the Chief Security Officer (OCSO) requires the replacement and modernization of existing CCTV systems at three headquarters facilities in Arlington, VA to improve physical security coverage and operational effectiveness. Remove existing CCTV cameras and head-end equipment and install replacement IP-based

Provide warranties and maintenance documentation
Deliver as-built drawings and operation manuals

Support IP-based monitoring.
Support Al-driven analytics.
Provide minimum 45 days video retention.
Support 4 CIF and 1080p resolution.
Operate continuously in support of facility security operations.
Meet applicable security and telecommunications requirements.
Comply with Section 889 telecommunications prohibitions.

opps.title: Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) PEO-SDA
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
SAM.GOV Special Notice Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) H9241525X0001
US Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) PEO SOF Digital Applications (SDA)

SECTION 1 – INTRODUCTION

1.1    USSOCOM Special Operations Forces Acquisition, Technology, and Logistics (SOF AT&L) seeks to accelerate the application of commercially derived software innovation into deployable warfighting capabilities in support of Special Operations Forces (SOF). Early identification of warfighter needs and continuous engagement with users during software development and fielding will support increased lethality and efficiency.

1.2    In order to support rapid fielding of tailored software capabilities, SOF AT&L seeks to award FAR and non-FAR contracts and agreements to non-traditional and traditional defense contractors to carry out prototype projects for Program Executive Office SOF Digital Applications (PEO SDA) that are directly relevant to enhancing the effectiveness of software intensive programs, platforms, components, algorithms, and processes. As such, under the authority of 10 USC § 3458 and DoD Class Deviation 2022-O0007, USSOCOM SOF AT&L releases this Commercial Solutions Opening. This CSO may result in an Other Transaction Authority (OTA) or a FAR-based contract.

1.3    This Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) will make awards to proposals for innovative, commercial technologies through multi-phased, competitive processes throughout 2024 and 2025. Awards may include prototypes as well as concept demonstrations, pilots, and agile software development activities that can incrementally improve commercial technologies, existing Government-owned capabilities, or concepts for defense application. Interested vendors should note that all of PEO SDA’s Programs of Record are being managed in accordance with DoDI 5000.87, the software acquisition pathway, for the timely acquisition of custom software capabilities developed for the DoD. Programs will require government

The limited number of available Government staff with these skill sets make it essential to find commercial sources to supply these services. opps.title: Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
***7/9/2026 Updated Solicitation Issue Date from 06/30/2026 to 08/25/2026.***
***7/8/2026 updated anticipated vehicle based off initial market research. Updated alternate contact information.***

USCIS has a requirement for Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE). This Operations and Maintenance Services requirement will assist USCIS to operate, optimize, enhance and maintain existing M365 services, onboard new M365 services, and plan and develop strategies for future service sustainment, not limited to, but including: Core productivity applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote); Collaboration and Communication (Microsoft Team, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Loop, Whiteboard); Security and Compliance (Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra ID, Intune, Rights Management and Information protection); Power Platform (Power Bi, Apps, Automate and Virtual Agents); Project and Task Management (Planner, To Do, List); Additional Services and Tools (Booking, Forms, Stream, Sway); Artificial Intelligence and Automation (Microsoft Copilot).  Assisting in planning and executing the rollout of Copilot /AI service in accordance with DHS and USCIS security and privacy policies as well as additional Microsoft 365 Services, USCIS may choose to adopt.   Additionally, USCIS needs support for M365 service integrations with both Microsoft and non-Microsoft Technologies, e.g., AirWatch, ServiceNow, Archive360, JAMF, and AgilQuest.

*Small business set aside to be determined through market research. USCIS intends to issue a Request for Information during May 2026.

opps.title: Information Technology Laboratory Commercial Solutions Openings (CSO)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description: The Engineer Research and Development Center (ERDC), Information Technology Laboratory (ITL) is issuing a Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) pursuant to 10 U.S.C. 3458 as implemented by RFO DFARS 212.7000. Under a CSO, the ERDC may competitively select proposals received in response to a general solicitation, similar to a Broad Agency Announcement (BAA), based on a review of proposal by scientific, technological, or other subject matter expert peers within the ERDC and/or other federal experts. This CSO will be used to acquire innovative products, services, or technologies, and under this CSO, all products, services, and technologies shall be treated as commercial items. Products, technologies, and services do not have to be ?commercially available? to be submitted in response to this solicitation. If the solution meets the requirements of the DFARS regulation, the solution is treated as commercial whereby the Contracting Officer will utilize commercial procedures to develop and execute the resultant award. This Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) contains broadly defined areas of interest for ERDC?s Information Technology Laboratory (ITL) and intends to obtain ?innovative? solutions or potential new capabilities in support of integrated digital transformation, advanced computing, secure and resilient systems, and data-driven decision-making to address complex defense and civil works challenges. Solutions may include existing technologies or procedures that are not currently in use that would enhance or streamline mission capabilities. More specific individual program requirements (IPRs) may be added in the future. The AOIs are intentionally broad in nature, generally have no known funding specifically available, and will be posted under this CSO on an open-continuous basis for one (1) year from the date of original posting. Any specific individual program requirements that are posted under the authority of this CSO will describe the desired end results, offer additi

opps.title: This procurement is for access to the comprehensive law library via UHD Hard Disk with a site license for unlimited use in support of Naval Consolidated Brig Miramar.
opps.agency: NAVSUP Fleet Logistics Center San Diego
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Publishers
opps.description:
Notice of Intent to Sole Source

THIS IS A NOTICE OF INTENT TO AWARD A SOLE SOURCE CONTRACT AND IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE QUOTES. Reference: Solicitation Number N00244-22-Q-0245. Naval Supply Systems Command (NAVSUP) Fleet Logistics Center San Diego (FLCSD) intends to award a firm fixed price contract under the authority of FAR 6.302-1(a)(2)(i)(A), only one source being available to meet the Government’s needs, to:

 RELX, Inc. - DBA: LexisNexis
 9443 Springboro Pike 
 Miamisburg, OH 45342-4425  

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this requirement is 511199; the business size standard is 500 (number of employees).

The objective of this sole source award is to issue a Firm Fixed-Price (FFP) contract for LexisNexis UHD, consisting of a multitude of legal resources, in support of the Naval Consolidated Brig Miramar (NAVCONBRIG Miramar).
SECNAVISNT 1640.9C and American Correctional Association standards requires our command to provide comprehensive resources for legal research on Military and Federal Law. Military Correctional Facility Miramar does not allow prisoners internet access so this is the only and or least expensive option. The UHD (Hard Disk) allows prisoners to search a multitude of legal databases on their time and without the requirement of staff supervision. If this system were not used, it would require many man hours to supervise each prisoner requiring access to legal materials. Additionally, the prisoners would not have ready use of legal materials, which may preclude them from meeting certain deadlines for clemency or trial.
The UHD is provided by Lexis Nexis with a site license for unlimited use by prisoners.

RELX, Inc. DBA: LexisNexis is the standard all Legal offices use and is the only electronic law library service available on a hard disk drive because prisoners cannot have internet. Only RELX was identified that offered this solution. The only other law library services available are onli

opps.title: AF12--Intent to Sole Source    Scientific Journal Supplement Publication Service -  Academic Emergency Medicine
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Publishers
opps.description:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, VA Boston Healthcare System, Center for Information Dissemination and Education Resources (CIDER) intends to negotiate on a sole source basis, pursuant to FAR 6.302-1, with the Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM) for scientific journal supplement publication services. 

CIDER requires services to produce a special journal supplement of peer-reviewed manuscripts in the Academic Emergency Medicine (AEM) Journal of Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM). The manuscripts written by VA Health Service Research and Development Service (HSR&D) and invited investigators/respondents will be focused on the  state of the art of emergency care in the VA healthcare system.  AEM/SAEM is uniquely qualified due to its emphasis on clinical trials, observational cohort studies, and economic or policy research, all of which are a part of health services research; its ranking as a top journal in the field; and its target audience and membership cohort of academic emergency physicians to produce a special journal supplement.
 
This notice of intent is not a request for competitive proposals; however, any responsible source who believes it is capable of meeting the requirement may submit a capability statement to the contracting office no later than Wednesday, February 23, 2022, 3:00 PM, EST.  Interest/capability statements may be sent to Shannon Hukriede at Shannon.Hukriede@va.gov. No telephone responses will be accepted. A determination not to compete the proposed requirement based upon the responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

opps.title: Department of Commerce (DOC) Cloud Services Provider Vehicle Strategy
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Data Processing/Hosting (cloud/data/AI infra)
opps.description:
1. Notice of Strategy

The Department of Commerce (DOC) is providing this notice to inform industry of its procurement strategy for the Cloud Vehicle Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA). This 10-year vehicle, with an estimated value of $4.105 Billion, is designed to provide native hyperscale cloud services across the Department.

2. Direct-to-CSP Approach

Due to highly specialized technical requirements, including massive compute elasticity (25,000+ concurrent vCPUs), proprietary 100+ Tbps global backbones, and specific hardware density for AI/ML and weather modeling, the Department is utilizing a Direct-to-CSP strategy. It is contemplated that to ensure mission success and direct engineering support, only Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) acting as the Cloud Service Provider (CSP) will be eligible for award.

3. Authority

This strategy is being executed via GSA eBuy under the authority of GSAR Subpart 538.71. Participation is restricted to authorized contractors holding a GSA Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) under SIN 518210C.

4. No Action Required

This notice is for informational purposes only to ensure transparency in the Department's long-term cloud acquisition planning. The Department is not seeking quotes, proposals, or feedback as part of this notice.

5. Points of Contact

Eric Olmstead, Contracting Officer: eric.olmstead@noaa.gov

opps.description:
This is a notice of intent, not a request for a quotation. A solicitation document will not be issued and quotations will not be requested.

This procurement is for a one-year (09/04/2026 – 09/03/2027) contract to maintain our current Fuji Medical (formerly TeraMedica)’s SynapseVNA product at the latest revision level and to provide technical support for that product, when necessary.  This package forms the core of the ResearchPACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System) maintained by RADIS/CC on behalf of NIH. Fuji Medical is the developer, sole licensor, and sole provider of technical support for the Synapse family of products. As such, it is in the only entity capable of providing the needed technical updates and support services.

This acquisition is being conducted using policies unique to the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 13 –Simplified Acquisition Procedures, Subpart 13.106-1(b)(1), For purchases not exceeding the simplified acquisition threshold (SAT), contracting officers may solicit from one source if the contracting officer determines that the circumstance of the contract action deem only one source reasonably available. Contracts awarded using FAR Part 13-Simplified Acquisition Procedures are exempt from the requirements of FAR Part 6-Competition Requirements.

Comments to this announcement, referencing this posting number, may be submitted to the NIH, Clinical Center, Office of Purchasing and Contracts, prior to the closing date specified in this announcement, to the attention of Ryssa Nix, Contract Specialist via email to Ryssa.nix@nih.gov by close date.

opps.title: Master Synch
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Video/Film Production
opps.description:
The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA.

The Video Lab at the NAVCC is tasked with the digitization of multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video. It was designed and built to substantially emulate a video production facility and uses an Evertz Master Sync Generator system. This system provides crucial reference video test signals for the video lab. It allows the VTR’s (video tape recorder) to lock on to a reference signal which provides the most optimal conditions for error free digitization of analog video and audio into the digital signal which is then used by the encoders to create archival files. The current system is obsolete and has been damaged beyond repair in certain areas such as the GPS antenna and cabling, which has suffered rodent damage.

The Library needs a contractor that will provide system hardware, installation and documentation for the replacement Master Sync system in the Video Lab at NAVCC.

Amendment 0001

The purpose of this amendment is to provide answers to questions. See attached Q&A document.

 The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA. The NAVCC is responsible for acquiring, preserving, and providing access to the world’s largest collection of films, television and radio broadcasts, videos, and sound recordings. 

The Video Lab at NAVCC is tasked with digitizing multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video using an Evertz Master Sync Generator system, which is currently obsolete and damaged beyond repair.

opps.title: Sources Sought - DCMA Blue List Program Support Services
opps.searchLabel: eBuy (all federal)
opps.description:
This is a sources sought posting to confirm the capabilities of GSA OASIS contractors to meet our Government requirement. NOTICE: This is NOT a solicitation, and no award will be made as a result of this Sources Sought Posting. 

The Government requirement is to hire 11 Full-Time Employees (FTE) in the following areas: One (1) Task Lead, two (2) Project Managers, two (2) IT Project Managers, two (2) IT Cybersecurity Specialists, one (1) Management Analyst, two (2) Contract Administrators, and one (1) Public Affairs/Media Outreach Specialist for one base year and one option year. This effort supports the agency's bona fide need to secure the sUAS supply chain, accelerate procurement timelines, and eliminate administrative barriers for warfighters seeking to acquire secure drone technology.

Attached is a DRAFT Performance Work Statement for industry review, and the Government is looking for feedback and any information that can help with creating an official solicitation. We are anticipating to release the official Request for Proposal in August, with award in September. Please provide any questions, feedback, or any insights possible to help make this possible. 

- How feasible are the requirements laid out in the Performance Work Statement (PWS) attached?
- How quickly, upon hiring, could you hire, onboard, and relocate (as applicable) to meet FTE needs?
- Does the information provided in the PWS give clear direction to the tasks being requested?
- Are there any recommended items or details that should be added in the PWS that may be unclear?

opps.title: Mexican Criminal Courts Telepresence
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
To permit more time for completion of Q&A, the due date for receipt of quotations is extended to July 24, 2026.

Judiciaries in Mexico’s border states (i.e. Baja California Norte, Sonora,Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo Leon, and Tamaulipas) face persistent security and operational challenges when hearing cases involving detainees imprisoned for TCO-related activities. Reliance on in-person hearings requires frequent inmate transport from detention facilities, creating elevated risks of escape, violence, intimidation of judicial personnel, and disruptions to proceedings. These risks contribute to hearing delays, case backlogs, and reduced judicial effectiveness at a time when TCO and FTO-related activity along the U.S.-Mexico border remains acute. The problem stems from limited access to secure, court-approved telepresence technology, uneven digital infrastructure, and procedural frameworks that have not adapted to evolving security threats. Addressing this issue is critical to ensure timely prosecution of high-impact cases.

This project will equip up to four state or federal judiciaries and their courtrooms in Mexico’s northern border states and the corresponding penitentiaries in those states or judicial districts with telepresence systems and secure digital communication platforms.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Mexican Criminal Courts Telepresence project aims to address the operational challenges faced by judiciaries in Mexico's northern border states, specifically Baja California Norte, Sonora, Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo Leon, and Tamaulipas. These challenges arise from the need for in-person hearings involving detainees linked to transnational criminal organizations (TCOs), which pose significant security risks during inmate transport. 

The project seeks to enhance judicial effectiveness by equipping up to four state or federal courtrooms and corresponding penitentiaries with secu
win_reasons: Small business set-aside
